content: The open-ended incremental search process that defined the incomplete partial findings of both Topic A and Topic B can be reframed as an emergent active learning agent memory system in itself, where each uncompleted search chunk functions as a retrievable working memory entry that encodes the agent's current curiosity-driven exploration state. This suggests that incomplete, fragmented research tracing outputs are not just messy intermediate data, but functional memory components that can be leveraged to improve next-generation active learning agent's curiosity alignment.
